summary: Tested multi-project tabs feature. All 14 test cases passed. Tab bar appears when first project is opened, supports multiple tabs, prevents duplicates, switches between tabs correctly, and closes tabs with proper nearest-tab switching logic.

features_tested.feature: Tab bar hidden initially (no tabs open)
Clicking project tile adds tab to tab bar
Tab bar appears after first project opened
Projects button visible in tab bar
Active tab has cyan highlight styling
Clicking Projects button returns to grid (tabs remain)
Opening second project creates second tab
Second tab becomes active when opened
Clicking inactive tab switches to that project
Clicking same project again does NOT create duplicate tab
Closing active tab switches to nearest remaining tab
Closing last tab returns to Projects grid
Tab bar disappears after closing last tab
Inactive tab close button has hover-dependent opacity (opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100)

critical_code_review_comments: Core System button only works if a project named 'Emanator Backend', 'Emanator', or with type 'core' exists - this is expected singleton behavior
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-project tabs feature fully tested and working. Tab bar uses data-testid='project-tabs-bar' and only appears when openProjectTabs.length > 0. Each tab has data-testid='project-tab-{uuid}' with activate and close buttons. Active tabs have cyan styling, inactive tabs have muted styling with hover-dependent close button visibility. Per-tab chat state is preserved via tabChatStateRef. Core System button opens existing core project as singleton tab (requires owner role and existing core project).
